mnemonics.title: Gram-Schmidt Steps
mnemonics.content: VPUN: Vector - Projection - Unit (normalize) - Next. Remember: Each new vector, subtract projections from all previous orthogonal vectors, then normalize.

mnemonics.title: Projection Formula
mnemonics.content: POP: Projection Of b onto a = (a·b/a·a) × a. Think: 'Pear Apple divided by Apple Apple' → 'a·b/a·a' multiplied by a.

mnemonics.title: Orthogonal Check
mnemonics.content: OZO: Orthogonal means Zero v One (dot product zero).

acronyms.expansion: Q = Orthonormal columns, R = Right (upper triangular)
acronyms.usage: QR decomposition uses Gram-Schmidt to decompose matrix A into Q and R
Numerically stable version of classical Gram-Schmidt

visualTips.visualization: Imagine shining a light perpendicular to vector u; the shadow of v on u is the projection. Drop a perpendicular line from v's tip to the line through u - where it hits is the projection point.
Picture coordinate axes (X, Y, Z) - each is perpendicular to the others, like the edges of a rectangular room meeting at a corner.
Think of building a staircase: first step is u₁, second step removes the part along step 1 to be perpendicular, third step removes parts along both previous steps, and so on.

analogies.analogy: Like decluttering a messy room: each new vector (item) is cleaned by removing components that overlap with already-organized items (previous orthogonal vectors).
Like a shadow on a wall - if you shine a light perpendicular to the wall, the shadow of a 3D object onto a line is its projection.
Like having a perfectly aligned team of soldiers facing cardinal directions (North, East, South, West) - all perpendicular to each other, all at equal 'height' (unit length).
